stories.headline: Bitcoin loses $77K as Hormuz oil shock and Fed jitters crush risk appetite
stories.tldr: BTC fell ~3% below $77,000 Tuesday after twice failing $80K, dragging ETH and SOL down as oil topped $100.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A weaker Coinbase premium plus rising oil signals US demand is fading just as the Fed wraps its meeting Wednesday — tightening odds, not cuts.
the_details: BTC opened $77,368 Tuesday and slid to $76,472 by 7:10 a.m. ET; ETH dipped to $2,278.
Brent held above $100 on a Strait of Hormuz standoff, complicating the Fed's inflation outlook.
Coinbase premium turned negative, signaling US sellers are leading the move down.

stories.headline: Aave-led coalition unveils $292M Kelp DAO recovery plan after cross-chain bridge exploit
stories.tldr: DeFi United, a coalition led by Aave, detailed a technical strategy Tuesday to restore the $292M rsETH backing drained April 19.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: First credible industry self-rescue since Ronin — sets the playbook for cross-chain restitution as 'DeFi is dead' takes hold among holders stranded on 20 chains.
the_details: Hacker drained 116,500 rsETH (~18% of supply) via Kelp's LayerZero bridge on April 19, the year's largest exploit.
Recovery plan covers wrapped ether stranded across 20 chains, the structural blocker to a clean unwind.
Kelp DAO breach edged out the $285M Drift Protocol hack from April 1, blamed on DPRK social engineering.

stories.headline: Robinhood Q1: crypto revenue craters 47% to $134M, prediction markets save the quarter
stories.tldr: Robinhood's Q1 crypto revenue fell 47% to $134M, but record prediction-market volume drove total revenue up 15% to $1.07B.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Retail rotation from spot crypto into election/event markets is now visible at the income-statement level — a structural shift, not a one-quarter blip.
the_details: Crypto revenue $134M, down 47% YoY; total revenue $1.07B, up 15%.
Prediction-market bets hit a record, offsetting the trading slowdown across spot crypto.
Earnings drop ahead of the Fed decision Wednesday, with Galaxy and Coinbase still on deck this week.

stories.headline: April crypto hacks hit $606M in 18 days — worst month since Bybit's $1.4B
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols lost $606M in the first 18 days of April, making it the worst hack month since the Bybit breach in February 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Attackers have pivoted from smart-contract bugs to private-key and signing-infrastructure compromises — security budgets aimed at Solidity audits are misallocated.
the_details: April losses are 3.7x larger than Q1 combined; YTD theft now ~$771.8M across 47 incidents.
Drift ($285M) and Kelp DAO ($292M) account for the bulk; both involved authorization or bridge layers, not contracts.
Cumulative crypto hack losses have crossed $17B over the past decade per industry trackers.

stories.headline: Visa teams with Tether co-founder Reeve Collins to build onchain banks
stories.tldr: Visa is partnering with Tether co-founder Reeve Collins on onchain banking rails aimed at under-banked users globally.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A network operator with billions of cards greenlighting stablecoin-native banking pulls regulated payment infra further into crypto's lane — neobanks should be nervous.
the_details: Project targets stablecoin payments infrastructure for under-banked populations rather than US/EU retail.
Reeve Collins co-founded Tether in 2014; this is his first major Visa-tier distribution deal.
Aligns with Q1 stablecoin market cap hitting $317B (>50% YoY) and tx volume of $28T.
